response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack and web-oriented engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires deep embedded/firmware C development for LAN switches, Linux kernel modification, and security technologies like MACsec and 802.1X — none of which appear on the resume. The hard requirement of native-level Japanese proficiency is not met (languages listed: English, Romanian, basic German). Limited C exposure (brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago) and no networking/switch security background make this a poor fit despite solid engineering credentials.
strengths: Long track record of senior software engineering across diverse stacks and domains
Some past C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Strong testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, craftsmanship) valuable for firmware QA collaboration
concerns: No native Japanese proficiency — a hard requirement for the role
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec, 802.1X, or Linux kernel modification experience
C programming is minimal and dated; recent work is TypeScript/Node/DLang web and platform engineering

key_factors.factor: Native Japanese language requirement not met
Minimal recent C and no Linux kernel work
